functions.description: Declared standalone table-valued functions (top-level `functions:` block). Each carries an explicit `namespace`, `kind`, and `config`. Source-attached functions live under their source's `functions:` instead.

include.description: Files (or glob patterns) whose `sources:` and optional `secrets:` are merged into this config before validation. Resolved relative to the declaring file.

observability.description: Optional observability block. Absent = today's behaviour (no activity log; export controlled by CLI flags).

secrets.description: Configured secret backends. Empty = use the built-in default chain (`env`, then OS keyring under `service=pawrly`).

semantic.description: Optional semantic layer. Absent = the layer is off and behavior is unchanged.

variables.description: Global (workspace-scoped) variable declarations, visible to every source. Per-source and fragment-file scopes layer on top during load.

version.description: Schema version. Only `1` is accepted in v1.

ActivityConfig.description: The `observability.activity:` block — one structured record per operation.

enabled.description: Master switch. Off by default, so no records are produced.

flush_interval.description: Maximum time a record waits in the buffer before a flush. `0` disables the timer (flush only on threshold / shutdown).

flush_threshold.description: Records buffered before a Parquet file is flushed.

partition_hours.description: `hr=` partition bucket width in hours (clamped to 1..=24).

redact_sql.description: How much of the submitted SQL to capture.

retention.description: Prune durable files older than this. Omit to keep all history.

ring_capacity.description: In-memory ring-buffer capacity for the `table` sink.

sinks.description: Which sinks receive records. Defaults to the `tracing` event sink.

store.description: Durable store directory for the `table` sink. Set to persist records as date/hour-partitioned Parquet; omit to keep only the in-memory ring.

oneOf.description: Emit each record as a `tracing` event (sink 1).
Expose records via the `system.activity` table (sink 2).

mode.description: Default cache mode applied to tables that don't declare their own.

namespace.description: Sub-directory of `storage` that isolates this workspace's cached data. When unset, a stable id is derived from the workspace path so different workspaces sharing the same `storage` root never collide on identical `schema.table` names. Set it explicitly to pin a stable namespace (e.g. across a moved directory) or to deliberately share a cache between workspaces.

storage.description: Filesystem path used as the cache root. When unset, it is derived from the Pawrly home directory as `$PAWRLY_HOME/cache` (default `~/.pawrly/cache`). A leading `~` in an explicit value expands to `$HOME`.

CachePolicy.description: Per-table cache mode declared in YAML.

oneOf.description: Default. No caching; every query hits the live source.
Read from the cache if fresher than `ttl`; else fetch live and write through.
Always read from the cache; a background task re-fetches every `every`.
Same as `refresh`, scheduled by cron expression.
Incremental: a `cursor_column` is used to fetch only newer rows on each refresh.

CredentialMethod.description: One way to collect a variable's value.

CumulativeWindow.description:
`RunningTotal` = unbounded from the series start (never resets). `GrainToDate { grain }` = resets at each calendar boundary (MTD/QTD/YTD). `Trailing { periods }` = rolling window of `periods` spine rows.

Deliberately NOT named `ToDate` — MetricFlow/Cube `to_date` means *period-to-date (resets)*, which is our `GrainToDate`, the opposite of a running total; the name is avoided to prevent a silent semantic inversion.

Defaults.description: Workspace-level defaults that apply unless overridden per source / table.

Dimension.description: A groupable attribute. `expr` is a raw SQL fragment over the base table.

grains.description: Valid time grains; meaningful only when `data_type == Time`.

duckdb_pool_size.description: Size of the DuckDB connection pool (in-memory).

max_concurrent_queries.description: Maximum concurrent queries served from a single engine instance.

memory_limit_bytes.description: Memory limit passed to DataFusion's memory pool. `None` = unlimited.

oneOf.description: `values: [start, end]` → `BETWEEN start AND end`.

FunctionArg.description: One declared argument. **List order is the positional call order**: required args precede optional/defaulted ones so a positional call is unambiguous.

default.description: Value used when the call omits this trailing arg. Mutually exclusive with `required`.

tool_arg.description: mcp only: wire name of the tool argument when it differs from `name`.

type.description: Column-vocabulary type (`varchar`, `int`, `bigint`, `double`, `bool`, `timestamp`, ...). Defaults to `varchar`. Drives CLI/MCP literal rendering and documentation; the HTTP pipeline itself is stringly.

FunctionColumn.description: One output column. `source` is either a JSONPath into each response row (`$.user.login`) or the literal `arg`, which injects the bound call argument of the same name.

FunctionDecl.description:
One function declaration — the single config wrapper for both the source-attached and standalone shapes.

Like [`TableDef`], `deny_unknown_fields` is intentionally absent: the `#[serde(flatten)] body` captures every kind-specific key (`endpoint`, `response`, `pagination`, `path`, `tool`, `rows_path`, ...). Top-level entries require `namespace` + `kind`; source-attached entries omit both plus `config` (all inherited). Placement and content are checked by the validator.

args.description: Ordered argument declarations — list order is the positional call order.

cache.description: Reserved; cache is inert in v1.

config.description: Standalone connection config (same shape as the matching source kind's `config`). Forbidden on attached functions.

kind.description: Execution backend — **standalone only**. Attached functions inherit it from the source kind.

name.description: Function name; a valid SQL identifier with no `__`.

namespace.description: SQL qualifier — **standalone only**. Source-attached functions inherit the source name as their namespace.

returns.description: Output columns; the schema is fixed at plan time. Non-empty (validated).

oneOf.description: Templated HTTP request → rows.
External MCP `tools/call` → rows.
Glob → file-metadata rows.
Human-readable text.
Line-delimited JSON.

allow_inline.description: Recognize the inline `-- pawrly: materialize <name>` directive on plain queries. Off by default; a `SELECT` that writes to disk is a footgun on a shared daemon, so enable it deliberately per workspace.

expr.description: Raw SQL fragment the aggregate is applied to.

filters.description: Measure-scoped predicates compiled into a `FILTER (WHERE ...)` clause.

MeasureAgg.description:
The aggregation kind for a [`Measure`].

Externally tagged: unit variants deserialize from a bare string (`agg: count_distinct`), and `Custom` from a map (`agg: { custom: { sql: "..." } }`).

Metric.description: A queryable business metric defined over one or more measures. Lives at the workspace level (not nested in a model) so it can compose measures across models. Evaluated *after* aggregation, as a projection over measure columns.

filter.description: Governed predicate AND-ed into every leaf measure of this metric (pushed to each leaf's `FILTER (WHERE …)`). Part of the metric's identity, unlike a caller's per-query `SemanticQuery.filters`.

name.description: Bare name; the member used to request it, e.g. `aov`. Must contain no `.` (reserved for `model.member`) and must not collide with a model name.

MetricKind.description: Externally tagged: `kind: { ratio: { … } }`. Deserialized via [`MetricKindRepr`] because `serde_yaml` only reads externally-tagged struct variants from `!tag` syntax, and the map form is the documented surface.

oneOf.description: `numerator / NULLIF(denominator, 0)`. Each operand is a measure (`orders.revenue`) or metric, with an optional per-side filter.

Scalar arithmetic over `{member}` references resolved from the aggregated output, e.g. `"({orders.revenue} - {orders.cost}) / NULLIF({orders.revenue}, 0)"`. A `{member | predicate}` token applies a per-token leaf filter.
Window aggregate over `measure` along the query's time grain, computed over the dense time spine. `agg` selects the window function.
Period-over-period: compare `measure` to itself `periods` grains back, aligned on the time spine so gaps don't misalign. `output` selects the prior value, the difference, or the growth ratio.
Part-of-whole: `measure` divided by a window aggregate of the same measure over `over` (a subset of the query's dimensions; empty = grand total). `agg` selects the denominator's window function.

otel.description: OpenTelemetry export (OTLP push and/or Prometheus pull).

OffsetOutput.description: What an `Offset` metric projects: the prior value, the difference from it, or the growth ratio.

Operand.description: The two YAML spellings of an [`Operand`].

dynamic_filter_pushdown.description: Off in v1; enabled once dynamic-filter pushdown is implemented.

logs.description: Bridge logs to OTel and export them.

prometheus.description: Prometheus pull endpoint, independent of OTLP push.

oneOf.description: OTLP over gRPC.
OTLP over HTTP/protobuf.

PreAggregation.description: A declared rollup: a pre-materialized grouping that the matcher can swap in for the base table when it covers the query.

measures.description: Measure names defined on the owning model.

refresh.description: Refresh cadence; how often the materializer rebuilds the rollup.

Relationship.description: A join from one model to another. `join_predicate` is a raw SQL fragment in which `this` aliases the declaring model and the target is referenced by its model name (e.g. `"this.customer_id = customers.id"`).

on.description: SQL join condition; `this` = the declaring model's alias.

target.description: Name of the model this relationship joins to.

RelationshipKind.description: Cardinality of a [`Relationship`], which picks the join type: `*-to-one` joins are `INNER`, `*-to-many` are `LEFT`.

max_unfiltered_rows.description: Hard cap on returned rows for any source that doesn't declare its own.

SafetyPolicy.description: Restrictions a source can place on its own tables to prevent accidental "scan the entire warehouse" queries.

require_at_least_one_filter.description: If true, refuse to scan with no filter at all.

require_filters_on.description: Columns that must appear in a filter; empty means no requirement.

required_predicates.description: Predicates AND-ed into every scan of the owning semantic model. May reference `${param:NAME}` placeholders, bound from `SemanticQuery::params` as escaped SQL literals at compile time (never interpolated as SQL fragments — see `pawrly-semantic`). Used for row-level security and always-on filters.

SecretsBackendDef.description: One secret backend in the resolution chain.

oneOf.description: Process environment.
A file on disk — a YAML map or a dotenv (`.env`) file. The `format` defaults to auto-detection from the file extension. Relative paths resolve against the directory of the declaring config file.
OS keyring under the given service.

Convenience chain: process environment, then the OS keyring (service `pawrly`), then a `.env` file in the config directory if one exists. A missing or insecure `.env` is skipped (with a warning), never fatal.
Detect from the file extension (`.env` → dotenv, else YAML).
A YAML map of `KEY: value`.
Dotenv-style `KEY=value` lines.

Segment.description:
A named, reusable set of filters defined on a [`SemanticModel`].

A segment is a label that expands into its predicates at compile time, referenced from a [`SemanticQuery`] as `model.segment` (e.g. `"orders.high_value"`). `describe_semantic_model` returns the model's segments so a client can discover and compose them, and they are auditable because the predicates live in trusted config — never in the request.

filters.description: Predicates AND-ed into the query when the segment is selected.

SemanticConfig.description: The `semantic:` config block — a set of business-named models.

include.description: Files (or glob patterns) that contain *only* semantic models, merged into `models` before validation. Each file is either a top-level `models:` list or a bare YAML sequence of model mappings — never sources, secrets, or other config. Resolved relative to the declaring file. The loader consumes this during multi-file assembly, so it is always empty on a fully-loaded `Config`.

metrics.description: Workspace-level metrics composed over model measures (ratios, derived expressions, windows). Dot-free names, queryable via the same `measures` field as `model.measure` members.

time_spine.description: Optional calendar table pinning the dense date axis window metrics run over. Absent, the compiler generates one at the query grain.

SemanticModel.description: A logical entity anchored on one physical table.

name.description: Business name, e.g. `orders`. Used as the member prefix.

pre_aggregations.description: Declared rollups. The rollup matcher rewrites the `FROM` clause to a materialized pre-agg when one covers the query; materialization itself is handled by the cache layer.

relationships.description: Joins to other models, walked by the compiler to answer queries whose members span models.

safety.description: Per-model guard rails. `required_predicates` (RLS) are AND-ed into every compiled query for this model.

segments.description: Named, reusable filter sets. A query references one as `model.segment` and the compiler expands it into its predicates before planning.

source.description: `"schema.table"` reference to a table registered in the `pawrly` catalog (schema = source name). Parsed by the compiler, not at deserialization time, so a malformed value surfaces as a query-time error rather than a config-load panic.

cache.description: Workspace-level cache override for this source.

config.description: Per-kind opaque config. Validated by the source builder when registered.

examples.description: SQL statements that must run successfully against this source. Run as live probes by `pawrly check` and surfaced through `describe_table` for the tables they reference.

from.description: Optional path to a YAML file containing the rest of this source's body. Resolved relative to the declaring file.

functions.description: Source-attached table-valued functions. They inherit this source's namespace (its name), `kind`, and connection `config`, so they omit `namespace`/`kind`/`config` of their own. Only valid on http/mcp/file sources.

raw_table.description: HTTP-shaped sources only: register a raw-HTTP table named after the source.

raw_table_safety.description: HTTP-shaped sources only: safety policy for the raw table.

safety.description: Workspace-level safety override for this source.

variables.description: Source-local variable declarations, visible to this source only (innermost in the scope chain). Resolved into `config`/`tables` at load.

wiki.description: Agent-facing usage notes for the whole source, surfaced through `describe_table` alongside any per-table `wiki`.

SourceKind.description:
Every supported `kind:` for a source declaration.

Two foundational backends — `file` (local files, or object storage via a `storage:` block) and `http` (any REST/GraphQL API) — plus a small set of first-class database / lakehouse builtins. The list is closed: adding a new kind requires a code change so the router can dispatch it. Kind aliases are matched case-insensitively at parse time but the canonical form is lowercase.

TableDef.description:
One per-table override / declaration. The body is opaque per-kind.

`deny_unknown_fields` is intentionally absent — this struct uses `#[serde(flatten)] body` to capture every kind-specific key (endpoint, path, format, params, …), so unknown fields are *expected* and routed into `body`.

wiki.description: Agent-facing usage notes, surfaced through `describe_table` alongside the schema.

TimeSpine.description: A declared calendar table for window metrics (`semantic.time_spine:`). Absent, the compiler generates a dense date axis at the query grain.

VarType.description: Underlying data type of a non-secret variable's value.

WindowAgg.description: The window function used by `Cumulative` and `Share`. (`Count`/distinct are deliberately excluded — re-aggregating per-period counts double-counts; use a measure-tier additive count instead.)
